entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: A staple food crop of arid and semi-arid regions of Asia and Africa.

entities.name: ARID AND SEMI-ARID REGIONS
entities.description: Environmental conditions where pearl millet is typically grown.

entities.name: ASIA
entities.description: Geographical region of origin and breeding for pearl millet populations.

entities.name: AFRICA
entities.description: Geographical region of origin and breeding for pearl millet populations.

entities.name: GENETIC DIVERSITY
entities.description: The variety of genetic characteristics assessed in pearl millet populations.

entities.name: SSR MARKERS
entities.description: Simple sequence repeat markers used to assess genetic diversity.

entities.name: CLUSTERING AND STRUCTURE ANALYSES
entities.description: Analytical methods used to evaluate genetic diversity based on SSR markers.

entities.name: INTROGRESSION
entities.description: The movement of genes from African origin breeding materials into Asian populations.

entities.name: GENE POOLS
entities.description: The total set of genes in a population, developed through introgression.

entities.name: DIALLEL MATING DESIGN
entities.description: A breeding design used to generate population hybrids through crossing.

entities.name: POPULATION HYBRIDS
entities.description: Offspring generated from crosses between different pearl millet populations.

entities.name: GENETIC DISTANCE
entities.description: A measure of genetic divergence between parental combinations.

entities.name: PANMICTIC MIDPARENT HETEROSIS
entities.description: The superiority of a hybrid over the average of its parents.

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD HETEROSIS
entities.description: The increase in grain yield of a hybrid compared to its parents.

entities.name: BREEDING SCHEMES
entities.description: Strategies used to develop high-yielding cultivars.

entities.name: HIGH-YIELDING CULTIVARS
entities.description: Pearl millet varieties developed for superior grain yield.
